For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. All products are sold strictly for in-vitro research and laboratory use.

Description

BPC-157 is a synthetic pentadecapeptide with the sequence GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, corresponding to a fragment of a protection protein originally isolated from human gastric juice. It has the empirical formula C62H98N16O22 and a molecular weight of approximately 1419.5 Da. The peptide is proline-rich and classified as a cytoprotective reference compound in preclinical literature.

BPC-157 has been used as a reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo studies of endothelial nitric oxide synthase (eNOS) signalling, angiogenic responses, and Src–Caveolin‑1–eNOS pathway modulation in vascular tissues. It is also employed in cell-based assays exploring cytoprotection and stress-response pathways in gastrointestinal and musculoskeletal models.

Supplied as an HPLC-verified lyophilized powder at >98% purity for research use. Typically reconstituted in sterile water or buffered aqueous media for in‑vitro applications, and stored lyophilized at 2–8°C, with aliquots maintained at −20°C after reconstitution, protected from light and moisture. Reference standard for in-vitro research on cytoprotective and angiogenic signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.

Frequently Asked Questions
What is the amino acid sequence and length of BPC-157?
BPC-157 is a 15-amino-acid peptide with the sequence GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, derived from a larger gastric protection protein.
Which in-vitro pathways is BPC-157 commonly used to study?
It is frequently used in vascular and cell-culture models investigating eNOS activation, VEGF/VEGFR2-related angiogenic signalling, and cytoprotective stress-response pathways.
Is the identity of BPC-157 supported by public chemical databases?
Yes. BPC-157 and its salt forms are listed in PubChem (e.g., CID 78169625 and CID 9941957) with standardized structural and physicochemical data.

For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. All products are sold strictly for in-vitro research and laboratory use.

Description

The CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin Blend is a two-component reference preparation containing CJC-1295 NO DAC (also termed Mod GRF(1–29)), a 29-residue GHRH analog, together with Ipamorelin, a synthetic pentapeptide growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHSR-1a) agonist. CJC-1295 NO DAC has a reported molecular weight around 3367.9 Da, while Ipamorelin (Aib-His-D-2-Nal-D-Phe-Lys-NH2) has a molecular weight of approximately 711.8 Da.

Typical research vials contain 10 mg total peptide comprising 5 mg CJC-1295 NO DAC and 5 mg Ipamorelin, corresponding to a 1:1 mass ratio. When normalized for molecular weight, this equates to an approximate molar ratio of ~1:4.8 (CJC-1295:Ipamorelin), reflecting the lower molecular weight and higher molar concentration of Ipamorelin at equal mass. The blend is used as a dual-peptide reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo models investigating GHRH receptor and GHSR-1a signalling in parallel.

The CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin Blend is supplied as a lyophilized powder of ≥99% HPLC purity suitable for reconstitution in aqueous media. Lyophilized vials are stored at −20°C protected from light, with reconstituted aliquots frozen or refrigerated as appropriate for the experimental schedule. Reference standard for in-vitro research on growth hormone-releasing hormone and growth hormone secretagogue receptor pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.

What peptides are present in the CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin Blend?
The blend contains CJC-1295 NO DAC (Mod GRF(1–29)), a GHRH analog, together with Ipamorelin, a selective GHSR-1a agonist pentapeptide.
What is the typical mass ratio and molar ratio of the components?
A common configuration is 5 mg CJC-1295 and 5 mg Ipamorelin (1:1 mass ratio), corresponding to roughly a 1:4.8 molar ratio (CJC-1295:Ipamorelin) given their different molecular weights.
How is this blend used in signalling studies?
It serves as a multi-peptide reference standard for experiments that concurrently interrogate GHRH receptor and ghrelin/secretagogue receptor signalling pathways.

For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. All products are sold strictly for in-vitro research and laboratory use.

Description

BPC-157 is a synthetic pentadecapeptide with the sequence GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, corresponding to a fragment of a protection protein originally isolated from human gastric juice. It has the empirical formula C62H98N16O22 and a molecular weight of approximately 1419.5 Da. The peptide is proline-rich and classified as a cytoprotective reference compound in preclinical literature.

BPC-157 has been used as a reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo studies of endothelial nitric oxide synthase (eNOS) signalling, angiogenic responses, and Src–Caveolin‑1–eNOS pathway modulation in vascular tissues. It is also employed in cell-based assays exploring cytoprotection and stress-response pathways in gastrointestinal and musculoskeletal models.

Supplied as an HPLC-verified lyophilized powder at >98% purity for research use. Typically reconstituted in sterile water or buffered aqueous media for in‑vitro applications, and stored lyophilized at 2–8°C, with aliquots maintained at −20°C after reconstitution, protected from light and moisture. Reference standard for in-vitro research on cytoprotective and angiogenic signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.

Frequently Asked Questions
What is the amino acid sequence and length of BPC-157?
BPC-157 is a 15-amino-acid peptide with the sequence GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, derived from a larger gastric protection protein.
Which in-vitro pathways is BPC-157 commonly used to study?
It is frequently used in vascular and cell-culture models investigating eNOS activation, VEGF/VEGFR2-related angiogenic signalling, and cytoprotective stress-response pathways.
Is the identity of BPC-157 supported by public chemical databases?
Yes. BPC-157 and its salt forms are listed in PubChem (e.g., CID 78169625 and CID 9941957) with standardized structural and physicochemical data.

BPC-157 is a synthetic pentadecapeptide with the sequence GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, corresponding to a fragment of a protection protein originally isolated from human gastric juice. It has the empirical formula C62H98N16O22 and a molecular weight of approximately 1419.5 Da. The peptide is proline-rich and classified as a cytoprotective reference compound in preclinical literature.

BPC-157 has been used as a reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo studies of endothelial nitric oxide synthase (eNOS) signalling, angiogenic responses, and Src–Caveolin‑1–eNOS pathway modulation in vascular tissues. It is also employed in cell-based assays exploring cytoprotection and stress-response pathways in gastrointestinal and musculoskeletal models.

Supplied as an HPLC-verified lyophilized powder at >98% purity for research use. Typically reconstituted in sterile water or buffered aqueous media for in‑vitro applications, and stored lyophilized at 2–8°C, with aliquots maintained at −20°C after reconstitution, protected from light and moisture. Description

The CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin Blend is a two-component reference preparation containing CJC-1295 NO DAC (also termed Mod GRF(1–29)), a 29-residue GHRH analog, together with Ipamorelin, a synthetic pentapeptide growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHSR-1a) agonist. CJC-1295 NO DAC has a reported molecular weight around 3367.9 Da, while Ipamorelin (Aib-His-D-2-Nal-D-Phe-Lys-NH2) has a molecular weight of approximately 711.8 Da.

Typical research vials contain 10 mg total peptide comprising 5 mg CJC-1295 NO DAC and 5 mg Ipamorelin, corresponding to a 1:1 mass ratio. When normalized for molecular weight, this equates to an approximate molar ratio of ~1:4.8 (CJC-1295:Ipamorelin), reflecting the lower molecular weight and higher molar concentration of Ipamorelin at equal mass. The blend is used as a dual-peptide reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo models investigating GHRH receptor and GHSR-1a signalling in parallel.

The CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin Blend is supplied as a lyophilized powder of ≥99% HPLC purity suitable for reconstitution in aqueous media. Lyophilized vials are stored at −20°C protected from light, with reconstituted aliquots frozen or refrigerated as appropriate for the experimental schedule. Reference standard for in-vitro research on growth hormone-releasing hormone and growth hormone secretagogue receptor pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.

A common configuration is 5 mg CJC-1295 and 5 mg Ipamorelin (1:1 mass ratio), corresponding to roughly a 1:4.8 molar ratio (CJC-1295:Ipamorelin) given their different molecular weights.

It serves as a multi-peptide reference standard for experiments that concurrently interrogate GHRH receptor and ghrelin/secretagogue receptor signalling pathways.
